(Details)

chapters.note: 站着喝酒而穿长衫的唯一的人。鲁迅自言这是他最喜欢的一篇。「孔乙己大约的确死了。」

chapters.label: 三

segs.zh: 秋天的后半夜，月亮下去了，太阳还没有出，只剩下一片乌蓝的天；除了夜游的东西，什么都睡着。华老栓忽然坐起身。擦着火柴，点上遍身油腻的灯盏，茶馆的两间屋子里，便弥满了青白的光。
segs.en: In the deep of an autumn night, the moon had gone down, but the sun had not yet risen; the sky was a vast, murky blue. Save for the creatures that roam by night, everything was asleep. Hua Laoshuan suddenly sat up. He struck a match and lit the oil-grimed lamp, and the two rooms of the teahouse were instantly filled with a pale, cold light.

segs.zh: ‘小栓的爹，你就去么？’是一个老女人的声音。里边的小屋子里，也发出一阵咳嗽。
segs.en: "Are you going, Little Shuan's father?" came the voice of an old woman. From the small room inside, a fit of coughing followed.

segs.zh: ‘唔。’老栓一面听，一面应，一面扣上衣服；伸手过去说，‘你给我罢。’
segs.en: Mm." Old Shuan responded as he listened, all the while buttoning his jacket; he reached out a hand and said, "Give it to me.

segs.zh: 华大妈在枕头底下掏了半天，掏出一包洋钱，交给老栓，老栓接了，抖抖的装入衣袋，又在外面按了两下；便点上灯笼，吹熄灯盏，走向里屋子去了。那屋子里面，正在窸窸窣窣的响，接着便是一通咳嗽。老栓候他平静下去，才低低的叫道：‘小栓……你不要起来。……店么？你娘会安排的。’
segs.en: Mother Hua fumbled under her pillow for a long time before producing a package of silver dollars, which she handed to Old Shuan. He took it, thrust it with trembling hands into his pocket, and patted it twice from the outside. Then, lighting his lantern and blowing out the lamp, he headed toward the inner room. From within came a rustling sound, followed by a fit of coughing. Old Shuan waited until it had subsided before calling out in a low voice: "Xiaoshuan... don't get up. The shop? Your mother will see to it."

segs.zh: 老栓听得儿子不再说话，料他安心睡了；便出了门，走到街上。街上黑沈沈的一无所有，只有一条灰白的路，看得分明。灯光照着他的两脚，一前一后的走。有时也遇到几只狗，可是一只也没有叫。天气比屋子里冷得多了；老栓倒觉爽快，仿佛一旦变了少年，得了神通，有给人生命的本领似的，跨步格外高远。而且路也愈走愈分明，天也愈走愈亮了。
segs.en: Hearing his son fall silent, Old Shuan assumed he had finally drifted off to sleep, so he stepped out of the house and onto the street. The street was pitch black and utterly deserted, save for the greyish-white ribbon of the path, which stood out clearly. The light of his lantern shone upon his feet as he walked, one stepping forward after the other. Occasionally he passed a few dogs, but not a single one barked. The air was much colder than it had been inside the house, yet Old Shuan felt invigorated; it was as if he had suddenly become a young man again, gifted with supernatural powers and the ability to bestow life, and his stride grew remarkably long and buoyant. Moreover, the road ahead seemed to grow clearer with every step, and the sky began to brighten.

segs.zh: 老栓正在专心走路，忽然吃了一惊，远远里看见一条丁字街，明明白白横着。他便退了几步，寻到一家关着门的铺子，蹩进檐下，靠门立住了。好一会，身上觉得有些发冷。
segs.en: Old Shuan was walking along, intent on his way, when suddenly he started in surprise. Far ahead, the T-shaped street lay clearly before him, blocking his path. He retreated a few paces, found a shop with its doors closed, and ducked into the shadow of the eaves, standing still against the door. After a long while, he began to feel a chill creeping over him.

segs.zh: ‘哼，老头子。’
segs.en: Hmph, you old geezer.
Quite happy, actually. . . .

segs.zh: 老栓又吃一惊，睁眼看时，几个人从他面前过去了。一个还回头看他，样子不甚分明，但很像久饿的人见了食物一般，眼里闪出一种攫取的光。老栓看看灯笼，已经熄了。按一按衣袋，硬硬的还在。仰起头两面一望，只见许多古怪的人，三三两两，鬼似的在那里徘徊；定睛再看，却也看不出什么别的奇怪。
segs.en: Old Quan gave another start, and opening his eyes, saw a few people passing before him. One of them even turned back to look at him; the features were indistinct, yet the gaze was like that of a starving man spotting food, a predatory glint flashing in his eyes. Old Quan looked at his lantern; it had already gone out. He pressed his hand against his pocket—the hard object was still there. He craned his neck to look around, but all he saw were many strange figures, wandering about in twos and threes like ghosts. He steadied his gaze to look again, yet could discern nothing else out of the ordinary.

segs.zh: 没有多久，又见几个兵，在那边走动；衣服前后的一个大白圆圈，远地里也看得清楚，走过面前的，并且看出号衣上暗红色的镶边。——一阵脚步声响，一眨眼，已经拥过了一大簇人。那三三两两的人，也忽然合作一堆，潮一般向前赶；将到丁字街口，便突然立住，簇成一个半圆。
segs.en: It was not long before a few soldiers were seen moving about over there; the large white circles on the front and back of their uniforms were clearly visible even from a distance, and as they passed by, one could also make out the dark red trim on their tunics. A sound of footsteps arose, and in the blink of an eye, a great crowd surged past. Those who had been walking in twos and threes suddenly converged, rushing forward like a tide; upon reaching the T-junction, they abruptly came to a halt, clustering into a semicircle.

segs.zh: 老栓也向那边看，却只见一堆人的后背；颈项都伸得很长，仿佛许多鸭，被无形的手捏住了的，向上提着。静了一会，似乎有点声音，便又动摇起来，轰的一声，都向后退；一直散到老栓立着的地方，几乎将他挤倒了。
segs.en: Old Shuan looked in that direction too, but could see only a mass of backs; necks were stretched out long, as if a great many ducks were being held and lifted by an invisible hand. After a moment of silence, there was a faint sound, and the crowd began to sway, then, with a roar, they all surged backward, scattering until they reached the spot where Old Shuan stood, nearly knocking him off his feet.

segs.zh: ‘喂！一手交钱，一手交货！’一个浑身黑色的人，站在老栓面前，眼光正像两把刀。刺得老栓缩小了一半。那人一只大手，向他摊着；一只手却撮着一个鲜红的馒头，那红的还是一点一点的往下滴。
segs.en: "Hey! Money first, goods second!" A man dressed entirely in black stood before Old Shuan, his eyes like two blades. They pierced Old Shuan, making him shrink to half his size. One of the man’s large hands was held out toward him, while the other clutched a bright red steamed bun, from which the red was still dripping, drop by drop.

segs.zh: 老栓慌忙摸出洋钱，扯抖的想交给他，却又不敢去接他的东西。那人便焦急起来，嚷道，‘怕什么？怎的不拿！’老栓还踌躇着；黑的人便抢过灯笼，一把扯下纸罩，裹了馒头，塞与老栓；一手抓过洋钱，捏一捏，转身去了。嘴里哼着说：‘这老东西……。’
segs.en: Old Shuan hurriedly fished out the silver dollar, his hand trembling as he reached out to pay, yet he dared not take the thing the man was offering. The man grew impatient and shouted, "What are you afraid of? Why don't you take it!" Old Shuan still hesitated; the black-clad man snatched the lantern, tore off its paper shade, wrapped the bun in it, and thrust it at Old Shuan. He grabbed the silver dollar with his other hand, gave it a squeeze, and turned to leave, muttering under his breath, "The old fool..."

segs.zh: ‘这给谁治病的呀？’老栓也似乎听得有人问他，但他并不答应；他的精神，现在只在一个包上，仿佛抱着一个十世单传的婴儿，别的事情，都已置之度外了。他现在要将这包里的新的生命，移植到他家里，收获许多幸福。太阳也出来了；在他面前，显出一条大道，直到他家中，后面也照见丁字有头破匾上‘古口亭口’这四个黯淡的金字。
segs.en: "Who is that medicine for?" Old Shuan seemed to hear someone ask, but he did not answer. His entire spirit was now focused on the parcel; he felt as though he were cradling a child representing the only hope for ten generations of his family, and everything else had been cast aside. He was now going to transplant this new life from the parcel into his own home and reap a harvest of great happiness. The sun had risen; before him lay a broad road leading straight to his door, and behind him, it shone upon the four dim, gilded characters "Gukouting" on the broken tablet at Dingzi Street.

segs.zh: 老栓走到家，店面早经收拾干净，一排一排的茶桌，滑溜溜的发光。但是没有客人；只有小栓坐在里排的桌前吃饭，大粒的汗，从额上滚下，夹袄也贴住了脊心，两块肩胛骨高高凸出，印成一个阳文的‘八’字。老栓见这样子，不免皱一皱展开的眉心。他的女人，从灶下急急走出，睁着眼睛，嘴唇有些发抖。
segs.en: When Old Shuan reached home, the shop had already been tidied up; the rows of tea tables were slick and gleaming. But there were no customers; only Little Shuan was sitting at a table in the back, eating. Large beads of sweat rolled down his forehead, and his padded jacket clung to his spine, where his two shoulder blades protruded sharply, forming the distinct shape of the character "eight." Seeing this, Old Shuan could not help but knit his brow, which had only just relaxed. His wife hurried out from the kitchen, her eyes wide, her lips trembling slightly.

segs.zh: ‘得了么？’
segs.en: Got it?

segs.zh: ‘得了。’
segs.en: That’s enough.

segs.zh: 两个人一齐走进灶下，商量了一会；华大妈便出去了，不多时，拿着一片老荷叶回来，摊在桌上。老栓也打开灯笼罩，用荷叶重新包了那红的馒头。小栓也吃完饭，他的母亲慌忙说：——‘小栓——你坐着，不要到这里来。’一面整顿了灶火，老栓便把一个碧绿的包，一个红红白白的破灯笼，一同塞在灶里，一阵红黑的火焰过去时，店屋里散满了一种奇怪的香味。
segs.en: The two of them went into the kitchen together and conferred for a while. Mother Hua then stepped out, returning shortly after with a withered lotus leaf, which she spread out on the table. Old Shuan opened the lantern shade and used the leaf to rewrap the red steamed bun. Little Shuan had just finished his meal, and his mother hurriedly called out, "Little Shuan—stay seated! Don't come over here." As she tended to the stove fire, Old Shuan shoved the emerald-green parcel and the tattered, red-and-white lantern into the hearth. A flare of crimson and black flame shot up, and a strange, pungent fragrance began to permeate the shop.

segs.zh: ‘好香！你们吃什么点心呀？’这是驼背五少爷到了。这人每天总在茶馆里过日，来得最早，去得最迟，此时恰恰蹩到临街的壁角的桌边，便坐下问话，然而没有人答应他。‘炒米粥么？’仍然没有人应。老栓匆匆走出，给他泡上茶。
segs.en: "Something smells good! What are you having?" It was Fifth Master Hunchback who had arrived. He spent his days in the teahouse, always the first to come and the last to leave. He shuffled over to a table in the corner facing the street and sat down, posing his question, yet no one answered him. "Is it fried rice porridge?" Still, no one replied. Old Shuan hurried out and poured him some tea.

segs.zh: ‘小栓进来罢！’华大妈叫小栓进了里面的屋子，中间放好一条凳，小栓坐了。他的母亲端过一碟乌黑的圆东西，轻轻说：——
segs.en: “Xiaoshuan, come in!” Mother Hua called Xiaoshuan into the back room, set a stool in the middle, and Xiaoshuan sat down. His mother brought over a small plate of something round and pitch-black, and said softly:—

segs.zh: ‘吃下去罢，——病便好了。’
segs.en: Eat it up—then your illness will be cured.

segs.zh: 小栓撮起这黑东西，看了一会，似乎拿着自己的性命一般，心里说不出的奇怪。十分小心的拗开了，焦皮里面窜出一道白气，白气散了，是两半个白面的馒头。——不多工夫，已经全在肚里了，却全忘了什么味；面前只剩下一张空盘。他的旁边，一面立着他的父亲，一面立着他的母亲，两人的眼光，都仿佛要在他身里注进什么又要取出什么似的；便禁不住心跳起来，按着胸膛，又是一阵咳嗽。
segs.en: Little Shuan picked up the black object and studied it for a moment. It felt as if he were holding his own life in his hands, and he felt a strange, indescribable sensation in his heart. With the utmost care, he pried it open. A wisp of white vapor escaped from the scorched crust, and once it dispersed, there lay two halves of a white flour bun. In no time at all, it was all in his stomach, though he had quite forgotten what it tasted like; before him lay only an empty plate. Beside him stood his father on one side and his mother on the other, their eyes fixed upon him as if they were trying to pour something into his very being or draw something out of it. He could not help but feel his heart begin to race; he pressed a hand to his chest, and another fit of coughing seized him.

segs.zh: ‘睡一会罢，——便好了。’
segs.en: Sleep for a while—and you'll be fine.

segs.zh: 小栓依他母亲的话，咳着睡了。华大妈候他喘气平静，才轻轻的给他盖上了满幅补钉的夹被。
segs.en: Xiaoshuan, obeying his mother, fell asleep coughing. Not until his breathing had calmed did Mother Hua softly tuck him in with the quilt, thick with patches, that covered him from head to foot.

segs.zh: 店里坐着许多人，老栓也忙了，提着大铜壶，一趟一趟的给客人冲茶；两个眼眶，都围着一圈黑线。
segs.en: The tavern was crowded. Old Shuan was busy too, carrying the large copper kettle back and forth to pour tea for the customers; both his eye sockets were rimmed with dark, sunken circles.

segs.zh: ‘老栓，你有些不舒服么？——你生病么？’一个花白胡子的人说。
segs.en: "Old Shuan, are you feeling a bit under the weather? Are you ill?" said a man with a grey beard.

segs.zh: ‘没有。’
segs.en: No.

segs.zh: ‘没有？——我想笑嘻嘻的，原也不像……’花白胡子便取消了自己的话。
segs.en: "None? — I meant to smile, but it didn't really look like one..." The grey-bearded man retracted his own words.

segs.zh: ‘老栓只是忙。要是他的儿子……’驼背五少爷话还未完，突然闯进了一个满脸横肉的人，被一件玄色布衫，散着纽扣，用很宽的玄色腰带，胡乱捆在腰间。刚进门，便对老栓嚷道：——
segs.en: Old Shuan was simply too busy. If his son were… Before the hunchbacked Fifth Master could finish his sentence, a man with a bloated, savage face suddenly burst in, wearing a black cloth tunic with its buttons undone, haphazardly cinched at the waist with a broad black sash. No sooner had he stepped through the door than he bellowed at Old Shuan:—

segs.zh: ‘吃了么？好了么？老栓，就是运气了你！你运气，要不是我信息灵。……’
segs.en: Had your fill? Feeling better? Old Shuan, you’re a lucky man! It’s your luck, I tell you—if I didn't have the inside track...

segs.zh: 老栓一手提了茶壶，一手恭恭敬敬的垂着；笑嘻嘻的听。满座的人，也都恭恭敬敬的听。华大妈也黑着眼眶，笑嘻嘻的送出茶碗茶叶来，加上一个橄榄，老栓便去冲了水。
segs.en: Old Shuan held the teapot in one hand, while the other hung respectfully at his side; he listened with a broad, beaming grin. The whole room sat listening just as respectfully. Mother Hua, her eyes ringed with dark circles, also came out with a beaming smile to set down the tea bowls and tea leaves, adding an olive to each, before Old Shuan went over to pour the boiling water.

segs.zh: ‘这是包好！这是与众不同的。你想，趁热的拿来，趁热吃下。’横肉的人只是嚷。
segs.en: “This is guaranteed! This is something out of the ordinary. Just think: brought to you piping hot, eaten while still piping hot.” The man with the rolls of fat on his face just kept on shouting.

segs.zh: ‘真的呢，要没有康大叔照顾，怎么会这样……’华大妈也很感激的谢他。
segs.en: "It’s true, if it weren’t for Uncle Kang looking after things, how could it have turned out like this..." Old Mrs. Hua said, thanking him with deep gratitude.

segs.zh: ‘包好，包好！这样的趁热吃下。这样的人血馒头，什么痨病都包好！’
segs.en: It’s a sure cure, a sure cure! Eat it while it’s hot like this. A human-blood bun like this can cure any case of consumption!

segs.zh: 华大妈听到‘痨病’这两个字，变了一点脸色，似乎有些不高兴；但又立刻堆上笑，搭赸着走开了。这康大叔却没有觉察，仍然提高了喉咙只是嚷，嚷得里面睡着的小栓也合伙咳嗽起来。
segs.en: At the words "consumption," Mother Hua’s expression flickered, looking somewhat displeased; but she immediately put on a smile and, making an excuse, walked away. Uncle Kang, however, did not notice, but kept shouting at the top of his lungs, his voice so loud that little Xiaoshuan, sleeping inside, joined in with a fit of coughing.

segs.zh: ‘原来你家小栓碰到了这样的好运气了。这病自然一定全好；怪不得老栓整天的笑着呢。’花白胡子一面说，一面走到康大叔面前，低声下气的问道，‘康大叔——听说今天结果的一个犯人，便是夏家的孩子，那是谁的孩子？究竟是什么事？’
segs.en: So your Little Shuan has had such a stroke of good luck. His illness is bound to be cured completely; no wonder Old Shuan has been grinning all day." As the man with the grizzled beard spoke, he moved over to Uncle Kang and asked in a fawning, low-voiced tone, "Uncle Kang—I heard that the prisoner executed today was the Xia family’s boy. Whose child is he, exactly? And what was it all about?

segs.zh: ‘谁的？不就是夏四奶奶的儿子么？那个小家伙！’康大叔见众人都耸起耳朵听他，便格外高兴，横肉块块饱绽，越发大声说，‘这小东西不要命，不要就是了。我可是这一回一点没有得到好处；连剥下来的衣服，都给管牢的红眼睛阿义拿去了。——第一要算我们栓叔运气；第二是夏三爷赏了二十五两雪白的银子，独自落腰包，一文不花。’
segs.en: Whose? Why, wouldn't it be Aunt Xia's son? That little brat!" Seeing everyone prick up their ears to listen, Uncle Kang became even more delighted; his facial muscles bulged and twitched as he spoke louder still. "If that little wretch didn't want his life, well, he didn't have to have it. As for me, I didn't get a single scrap of benefit this time; even the clothes stripped off his back were snatched up by Red-Eye A-Yi, the jailer. First of all, we have to count Brother Shuan as lucky; and secondly, Master Third Xia handed over twenty-five taels of gleaming white silver, which went straight into his own pocket without a single copper spent.

segs.zh: 小栓慢慢的从小屋子走出，两手按了胸口，不住的咳嗽；走到灶下，盛出一碗冷饭，泡上热水，坐下便吃。华大妈跟着他走，轻轻的问道，‘小栓你好些么？——你仍旧只是肚饿？……’
segs.en: Xiaoshuan walked slowly out of the small room, pressing both hands to his chest, coughing incessantly. He went to the stove, ladled out a bowl of cold rice, poured hot water over it, and sat down to eat. Mother Hua followed him out and asked softly, "Are you feeling any better, Xiaoshuan? — Are you still just feeling hungry?..."

segs.zh: ‘包好，包好！’康大叔瞥了小栓一眼，仍然回过脸，对众人说，‘夏三爷真是乖角儿，要是他不先告官，连他满门抄斩。现在怎样？银子！——这小东西也真不成东西！关在牢里，还要劝牢头造反。’
segs.en: Wrapped up, all wrapped up!" Uncle Kang glanced at Little Shuan, then turned back to the others. "That Third Master Xia is a sharp one. If he hadn't reported it to the authorities first, his entire family would have been wiped out. And look at him now—he’s got the silver! As for this little wretch, he really is a piece of trash. Locked up in the cell, and still trying to incite the jailer to rebel.

segs.zh: ‘阿呀，那还了得。’坐在后排的一个二十多岁的人，很现出气愤模样。
segs.en: “Oh, that’s simply outrageous!” A man in his twenties sitting in the back row appeared quite incensed.

segs.zh: ‘你要晓得红眼睛阿义是去盘盘底细的，他却和他攀谈了。他说：这大清的天下是我们大家的。你想：这是人话么？红眼睛原知道他家里只有一个老娘，可是没有料到他竟会那么穷，榨不出一点油水，已经气破肚皮了。他还要老虎头上搔痒，便给他两个嘴巴！’
segs.en: You must understand that Red-Eyed Ah Yi went there specifically to sound him out, yet he actually tried to strike up a conversation with him. He said, "This Great Qing empire belongs to all of us." Just think: is that the sort of thing a human being would say? Red-Eyed Ah Yi already knew he had only an old mother at home, but he never expected him to be so wretchedly poor that not a drop of profit could be squeezed out of him, which had already made him burst with rage. And then, as if scratching the head of a tiger, he had the gall to talk back, so he gave him a couple of slaps!

segs.zh: ‘义哥是一手好拳棒，这两下，一定够他受用了。’壁角的驼背忽然高兴起来。
segs.en: “Brother Yi is quite the master of the fist and staff; those two blows will surely be enough to keep him well-occupied.” The hunchback in the corner suddenly brightened.

segs.zh: ‘他这贱骨头打不怕，还要说可怜可怜哩。’
segs.en: That wretch has a hide too tough to be broken by a beating, and he still has the gall to whine about being pitied.

segs.zh: 花白胡子的人说，‘打了这种东西，有什么可怜呢？’
segs.en: The man with the grizzled beard said, "What is there to pity about beating a thing like that?"

segs.zh: 康大叔显出看他不上的样子，冷笑着说，‘你没有听清我的话；看他神气，是说阿义可怜哩！’
segs.en: Uncle Kang looked at him with undisguised contempt and said with a cold sneer, "You didn't hear me clearly; to judge by his expression, he was saying that A-Yi is a pitiable wretch!"

segs.zh: 听着的人的眼光，忽然有些板滞；话也停顿了，小栓已经吃完饭，吃得满身流汗，头上都冒出蒸气来。
segs.en: The listeners’ eyes suddenly glazed over; the conversation faltered. Xiaoshuan had finished his meal, sweating all over, steam rising even from the top of his head.

segs.zh: ‘阿义可怜——疯话，简直是发了疯了。’花白胡子恍然大悟似的说。
segs.en: "Poor Ah Yi—what madness, he’s simply gone mad," the man with the graying beard remarked, as if suddenly enlightened.

segs.zh: ‘发了疯了。’二十多岁的人也恍然大悟的说。
segs.en: "He's gone mad," a man in his twenties remarked, as if suddenly enlightened.

segs.zh: 店里的坐客，便又现出活气，谈笑起来。小栓也趁着热闹，拼命咳嗽；康大叔走上前，拍他肩膀说：——
segs.en: The patrons in the tavern came back to life, laughing and talking once more. Taking advantage of the commotion, Little Shuan began to cough for all he was worth; Uncle Kang stepped forward and, patting him on the shoulder, said: —

segs.zh: ‘包好！小栓——你不要这么咳。包好！’
segs.en: Wrap it up! Little Shuan—don’t cough like that. Wrap it up!

segs.zh: ‘疯了。’驼背五少爷点着头说。
segs.en: "He's gone mad," the fifth young master with the hunchback said, nodding.

segs.zh: 西关外靠着城根的地面，本是一块官地；中间歪歪斜斜一条细路，是贪走便道的人，用鞋底造成的，但却成了自然的界限。路的左边，都埋着死刑和瘦毙的人，右边是穷人的丛冢。两面都已埋到层层叠叠，宛然阔人家里祝寿时候的馒头。
segs.en: The ground skirting the city wall outside the West Gate was originally public land. A crooked, narrow path ran through the middle, worn into the earth by the soles of those eager to take a shortcut, and it had become a natural boundary. To the left of the path lay the buried remains of those executed or starved to death; to the right was a cluster of graves for the poor. On both sides, the mounds were piled layer upon layer, looking for all the world like the steamed buns stacked up for a birthday celebration in a wealthy household.

segs.zh: 这一年的清明，分外寒冷；杨柳才吐出半粒米大的新芽。天明未久，华大妈已在右边的一坐新坟前面，排出四碟菜，一碗饭，哭了一场。化过纸，呆呆的坐在地上；仿佛等候什么似的，但自己也说不出等候什么。微风起来，吹动他短发，确乎比去年白得多了。
segs.en: That year, the Qingming Festival was exceptionally cold; the willows had only just put forth buds the size of half a grain of rice. It was not long after daybreak when Mother Hua, standing before a fresh grave on the right, laid out four dishes and a bowl of rice, and had a good cry. After burning the paper money, she sat blankly on the ground, as if waiting for something—though she herself could not have said what. A breeze sprang up, stirring her short hair, which had indeed turned much whiter than it was the year before.

segs.zh: 小路上又来了一个女人，也是半白头发，褴褛的衣裙；提一个破旧的朱漆圆篮，外挂一串纸锭，三步一歇的走。忽然见华大妈坐在地上看他，便有些踌躇，惨白的脸上，现出些羞愧的颜色；但终于硬着头皮，走到左边的一坐坟前，放下了篮子。
segs.en: Another woman approached along the path, also with streaks of grey in her hair and wearing ragged clothes. She carried a battered, round, vermilion-lacquered basket, from which hung a string of paper ingots, and she walked with a faltering gait, pausing every three steps. Suddenly spotting Mother Hua sitting on the ground watching her, she hesitated, a look of shame appearing on her pallid face; but finally, steeling herself, she walked over to a grave on the left and set down her basket.

segs.zh: 那坟与小栓的坟，一字儿排着，中间只隔一条小路。华大妈看他排好四碟菜，一碗饭，立着哭了一通，化过纸锭；心里暗暗地想，‘这坟里的也是儿子了。’那老女人徘徊观望了一回，忽然手脚有些发抖，跄跄踉踉退下几步，瞪着眼只是发怔。
segs.en: That grave and Little Shuan’s grave stood in a row, separated only by a narrow path. After Mother Hua had set out the four dishes and the bowl of rice, she stood there and wept for a while, then burned some paper money. In her heart, she thought quietly, "This one in the grave is someone’s son, too." The old woman lingered, looking around, when suddenly her hands and feet began to tremble. She stumbled back a few paces, staring blankly, lost in a daze.

segs.zh: 华大妈见这样子，生怕他伤心到快要发狂了；便忍不住立起身，跨过小路，低声对他说，‘你这位老奶奶不要伤心了，——我们还是回去罢。’
segs.en: Seeing the state he was in, Old Mrs. Hua grew terrified that he might be driven to the brink of madness by his grief. Unable to restrain herself, she rose, crossed the narrow path, and whispered to her, "Do not grieve so, old lady—let us be going home."

segs.zh: 那人点一点头，眼睛仍然向上瞪着；也低声吃吃的说道，‘你看。——看这是什么呢？’
segs.en: The man nodded, his eyes still fixed in an upward stare, and said in a low, tittering voice, "Look. — Look at this, what could it be?"

segs.zh: 华大妈跟了他指头看去，眼光便到了前面的坟，这坟上草根还没有全合，露出一块一块的黄土，煞是难看。再往上仔细看时，却不觉也吃一惊；——分明有一圈红白的花，围着那尖圆的坟顶。
segs.en: Mother Hua followed the direction of his finger, her gaze landing on the grave ahead. The grass roots had not yet fully covered it, leaving patches of yellow earth exposed—a truly unsightly sight. But as she looked more closely, she could not help but start in surprise: there, circling the rounded peak of the grave, was a wreath of red and white flowers.

segs.zh: 他们的眼睛都已老花多年了，但望这红白的花，却还能明白看见。花也不很多，圆圆的排成一个圈，不很精神，倒也整齐。华大妈忙看他儿子和别人的坟，却只有不怕冷的几点青白小花，零星开着；便觉得心里忽然感到一种不足和空虚，不愿意根究。那老女人又走近几步，细看了一遍，自言自语的说，‘这没有根，不像自己开的！这地方有谁来呢？孩子不会来玩；——亲戚本家早不来了。——这是怎么一回事呢？’他想了又想，忽又流下泪来，大声说道：——
segs.en: Their eyes had long been clouded by age, yet they could see these red and white flowers clearly enough. There were not many of them, arranged in a neat, round circle, though they looked rather listless. Mother Hua turned hurriedly to look at her son’s grave and the others nearby, but there were only a few small, cold-resistant bluish-white flowers blooming sparsely here and there; she felt a sudden sense of lack and emptiness in her heart, though she was unwilling to probe the reason why. The old woman stepped a little closer, examined the flowers once more, and muttered to herself, “There are no roots here; they didn’t grow by themselves! Who would come to a place like this? Children wouldn’t come here to play—and as for relatives or kin, they stopped coming long ago. What does this mean?” She thought and thought, until suddenly the tears began to fall, and she cried out aloud:—

segs.zh: ‘瑜儿，他们都冤枉了你，你还是忘不了，伤心不过，今天特意显点灵，要我知道么？’他四面一看，只见一只乌鸦，站在一株没有叶的树上，便接着说，‘我知道了。——瑜儿，可怜他们坑了你，他们将来总有报应，天都知道；你闭了眼睛就是了。——你如果真在这里，听到我的话，——便教这乌鸦飞上你的坟顶，给我看罢。’
segs.en: Yuer, they have all wronged you. You still cannot forget, you are too heartbroken, and you have come today to show your spirit so that I might know, haven't you?" He looked all around and saw only a crow perched on a leafless tree, then continued, "I know. Yuer, it is a pity they destroyed you, but they will surely meet their retribution in the end; Heaven knows it all. You may close your eyes now. If you are truly here, if you can hear my words, then—let this crow fly onto the top of your grave to show me.

segs.zh: 微风早经停息了；枯草支支直立，有如铜丝。一丝发抖的声音，在空气中愈颤愈细，细到没有，周围便都是死一般静。两人站在枯草丛里，仰面看那乌鸦；那乌鸦也在笔直的树枝间，缩着头，铁铸一般站着。
segs.en: The breeze had long since died away; the withered grass stood stiff and straight, like strands of copper wire. A trembling sound vibrated through the air, growing thinner and thinner until it vanished altogether, leaving a deathly stillness all around. The two of them stood amidst the withered grass, faces upturned to watch the crow; and the crow, too, perched among the vertical branches with its head tucked in, standing as still as if cast in iron.

segs.zh: 许多的工夫过去了；上坟的人渐渐增多，几个老的小的，在土坟间出没。
segs.en: A long time passed; the number of people visiting the graves gradually increased, several old and young figures appearing and disappearing among the earthen mounds.

segs.zh: 华大妈不知怎的，似乎卸下了一挑重担，便想到要走；一面劝着说，‘我们还是回去罢。’
segs.en: Somehow, Mother Hua felt as if a heavy load had been lifted from her shoulders, and she thought it was time to go. "We had better be getting back," she urged.

segs.zh: 那老女人叹一口气，无精打采的收起饭菜；又迟疑了一刻，终于慢慢地走了。嘴里自言自语的说，‘这是怎么一回事呢？……’
segs.en: The old woman sighed, listlessly cleared away the meal, and then, after a moment’s hesitation, finally walked away, moving slowly. She muttered to herself, "Whatever can be the meaning of this?..."

segs.zh: 他们走不上二三十步远，忽听得背后‘哑——’的一声大叫；两个人都竦然的回过头，只见那乌鸦张开两翅，一挫身，直向着远处的天空，箭也似的飞去了。
segs.en: They had walked no more than twenty or thirty paces when suddenly, from behind them, came a sharp, raucous "Caw!" Both of them started and looked back, only to see the crow spread its wings, give a sudden jerk of its body, and shoot off into the distant sky like an arrow.

segs.zh: （一九一九年四月。）

segs.en: This work may also be in the public domain in countries and regions where the copyright term is longer for domestic works but shorter for foreign ones.

segs.zh: 据《中华人民共和国著作权法释义》，法人作品应满足三点要求：（1）由法人（或其他组织，以下简称“法人”）主持创作，而非工作人员自发进行；（2）创作思想及表达方式体现法人意志；（3）由法人负责，而非执笔者。（详情）
segs.en: According to the Explanation of the Copyright Law of the People’s Republic of China, a work by a legal entity must satisfy three requirements: (1) it is created under the auspices of a legal entity (or other organization, hereafter referred to as a "legal entity") rather than spontaneously by its staff; (2) the creative intent and mode of expression reflect the will of the legal entity; and (3) the legal entity, rather than the individual writer, bears responsibility for the work. (Details)

chapters.note: 人血馒头与一座坟。两条线在坟场交汇，鲁迅在瑜儿的坟上添了一个花环——给那时仍不肯消极的自己。

segs.zh: ‘没有声音，——小东西怎了？’
segs.en: No sound—what's wrong with the little thing?

segs.zh: 红鼻子老拱手里擎了一碗黄酒，说着，向间壁努一努嘴。蓝皮阿五便放下酒碗，在他脊梁上用死劲的打了一掌，含含糊糊嚷道：
segs.en: Old Gong of the Red Nose held a bowl of yellow rice wine in his hand as he spoke, gesturing with his chin toward the room next door. Blue-jacketed Ah Wu set down his own bowl, slapped him hard on the back, and shouted incoherently:

segs.zh: ‘你……你你又在想心思。……’
segs.en: You... you you’re brooding again...

segs.zh: 原来鲁镇是僻静地方，还有些古风：不上一更，大家便都关门睡觉。深更半夜没有睡的只有两家：一家是咸亨酒店，几个酒肉朋友围着柜台，吃喝得正高兴；一家便是间壁的单四嫂子，他自从前年守了寡，便须专靠着自己的一双手纺出棉纱来，养活他自己和他三岁的儿子，所以睡的也迟。
segs.en: Lu Town was a secluded place that still held to some of its old-fashioned ways: long before the first watch of the night, everyone would bolt their doors and go to sleep. Deep in the dead of night, only two establishments remained awake: one was the Xianheng Tavern, where a few boon companions were gathered around the counter, drinking and making merry; the other was the house next door, belonging to Widow Shan Si. Ever since she had been widowed the year before last, she had had to rely solely on the strength of her own hands, spinning cotton yarn to support herself and her three-year-old son, which was why she, too, slept so late.

segs.zh: 这几天，确凿没有纺纱的声音了。但夜深没有睡的既然只有两家，这单四嫂子家有声音，便自然只有老拱们听到，没有声音，也只有老拱们听到。
segs.en: For the past few days, there had been no sound of spinning, that was certain. But since there were only two households that stayed awake late into the night, if there was a sound from Widow Shan Si’s place, it was only the night-watchmen who heard it; and if there was no sound, it was also only the night-watchmen who heard that.

segs.zh: 老拱挨了打，仿佛很舒服似的喝了一大口酒，呜呜的唱起小曲来。
segs.en: Lao Gong, having taken his beating, drank a large gulp of wine as if he were quite comfortable, and began to hum a little tune.

segs.zh: 这时候，单四嫂子正抱着他的宝儿，坐在床沿上，纱车静静的立在地上。黑沈沈的灯光，照着宝儿的脸，绯红里带一点青。单四嫂子心里计算：神签也求过了，愿心也许过了，单方也吃过了，要是还不见效，怎么好？——那只有去诊何小仙了。但宝儿也许是日轻夜重，到了明天，太阳一出，热也会退，气喘也会平的：这实在是病人常有的事。
segs.en: At this moment, Sister Shan Si was sitting on the edge of the bed, holding her Bao'er in her arms; the spinning wheel stood silent on the floor. The dim, murky lamplight shone on the child’s face, which was flushed a deep crimson with a hint of greyish blue. Sister Shan Si pondered: she had already consulted the oracle, made her vows, and tried the folk remedies—what was to be done if none of these proved effective? The only remaining course would be to see He Xiaoxian. Yet, perhaps Bao'er was simply better by day and worse by night; by tomorrow, once the sun rose, the fever might break and the wheezing subside: this was, after all, a common enough occurrence for the sick.

segs.zh: 单四嫂子是一个粗笨女人，不明白这‘但’字的可怕：许多坏事固然幸亏有了他才变好，许多好事却也因为有了他都弄糟。夏天夜短，老拱们呜呜的唱完了不多时，东方已经发白；不一会，窗缝里透进了银白色的曙光。
segs.en: Sister Shan Si was a clumsy, slow-witted woman who did not understand the terror of that little word "but": true, many a bad situation is salvaged thanks to it, but many a good one is ruined by it just the same. Summer nights are short; the old topers had barely finished their droning songs before the east began to pale, and soon, a silvery light seeped through the cracks in the window.

segs.zh: 单四嫂子等候天明，却不像别人这样容易，觉得非常之慢，宝儿的一呼吸，几乎长过一年。现在居然明亮了；天的明亮，压倒了灯光，——看见宝儿的鼻翼，已经一放一收的扇动。
segs.en: For Mother Shan, waiting for dawn was not as easy as it was for others; it felt agonizingly slow, and every breath Bao’er drew seemed to last a whole year. At last, however, it grew light; the brightness of the day eclipsed the lamp, and she saw the wings of Bao’er’s nose beginning to flutter, expanding and contracting.

segs.zh: 单四嫂子知道不妙，暗暗叫一声‘阿呀！’心里计算：怎么好？只有去诊何小仙这一条路了。他虽然是粗笨女人，心里却有决断，便站起身，从木柜子里掏出每天节省下来的十三个小银元和一百八十铜钱，都装在衣袋里，锁上门，抱着宝儿直向何家奔过去。
segs.en: Sister Shan Si sensed that something was wrong and let out a silent cry of "Oh!" She turned it over in her mind: what was to be done? There was no other path but to consult He Xiaoxian. Though she was a simple, unrefined woman, she had a resolute heart; she stood up, took the thirteen small silver coins she had saved over the days and the one hundred and eighty copper cash from the wooden cabinet, tucked them all into her pocket, locked the door, and clutching Bao’er to her, ran straight toward the He household.

segs.zh: 天气还早，何家已经坐着四个病人了。他摸出四角银元，买了号签，第五个便轮到宝儿。何小仙伸开两个指头按脉，指甲足有四寸多长，单四嫂子暗地纳罕，心里计算：宝儿该有活命了。但总免不了着急，忍不住要问，便局局促促的说：——
segs.en: It was still early, but already four patients were waiting at the He residence. She took out a four-jiao silver coin, bought a number, and Bao’er was fifth in line. He Xiaoxian stretched out two fingers to take the pulse; his fingernails were a good four inches long. Shan Si Sao secretly marveled, calculating to herself: Bao’er should have a chance at life now. Yet she could not help feeling anxious, and unable to restrain herself, she asked in a flustered, hurried voice: —

segs.zh: ‘先生，——我家的宝儿什么病呀？’
segs.en: Sir, tell me—what is wrong with my precious child?

segs.zh: ‘他中焦塞着。’
segs.en: His middle burner is obstructed.

segs.zh: ‘不妨事么？他……’
segs.en: No harm in that? He……

segs.zh: ‘先去吃两帖。’
segs.en: Go and take two doses first.

segs.zh: ‘他喘不过气来，鼻翅子都扇着呢。’
segs.en: He was gasping for breath, his nostrils flaring with every intake of air.

segs.zh: ‘这是火克金……’
segs.en: This is Fire overcoming Metal…

segs.zh: 何小仙说了半句话，便闭上眼睛；单四嫂子也不好意思再问。在何小仙对面坐着的一个三十多岁的人，此时已经开好一张药方，指着纸角上的几个字说道：——
segs.en: He Xiaoxian spoke half a sentence, then closed his eyes; Mother Shan, feeling it would be improper to press him further, said nothing. A man of about thirty, sitting opposite He Xiaoxian, had by now finished writing out a prescription. He pointed to a few characters in the corner of the paper and said:

segs.zh: ‘这第一味保婴活命丸，须是贾家济世老店才有！’
segs.en: This first dose of Life-Saving Infant Pills can only be had at the Jia family’s philanthropic old apothecary!

segs.zh: 单四嫂子接过药方，一面走，一面想。他虽是粗笨女人，却知道何家与济世老店与自己的家，正是一个三角点；自然是买了药回去便宜了。于是又径向济世老店奔过去。店伙也翘了长指甲慢慢的看方，慢慢的包药。单四嫂子抱了宝儿等着；宝儿忽然擎起小手来，用力拔他散乱着的一绺头发，这是从来没有的举动，单四嫂子怕得发怔。
segs.en: Sister Shan Si took the prescription, walking and thinking as she went. Though a simple, unrefined woman, she knew that the He family, the Jishi Pharmacy, and her own home formed a perfect triangle; it would naturally be cheaper to buy the medicine on her way back. And so, she headed straight for the Jishi Pharmacy. The shop assistant, sporting long fingernails, examined the prescription slowly and wrapped the medicine just as slowly. Sister Shan Si stood waiting, clutching Bao’er in her arms. Suddenly, Bao’er raised his small hand and tugged forcefully at a loose strand of her hair—a gesture he had never made before. Terrified, Sister Shan Si stood rooted to the spot, dazed with fear.

segs.zh: 太阳早出了。单四嫂子抱了孩子，带着药包，越走觉得越重；孩子又不住的挣扎，路也觉得越长。没奈何坐在路旁一家公馆的门槛上，休息了一会，衣服渐渐的冰着肌肤，才知道自己出了一身汗；宝儿却仿佛睡着了。他再起来慢慢地走，仍然支撑不得，耳朵边忽然听得人说：——
segs.en: The sun was already high. Carrying her child and the package of medicine, Sister Shan found the burden growing heavier with every step; the child kept struggling, and the road seemed to stretch out endlessly. With no other choice, she sat down on the threshold of a mansion by the roadside to rest for a while. As her clothes began to chill her skin, she realized she was drenched in sweat; Bao’er, however, seemed to have fallen asleep. When she rose to continue her slow walk, she still could not hold up, when suddenly she heard someone say by her ear:——

segs.zh: ‘单四嫂子我替你抱勃啰！’似乎是蓝皮阿五的声音。
segs.en: "Sister Shan Si, let me carry the baby for you!" It sounded like the voice of Blue-skinned A-Wu.

segs.zh: 他抬头看时，正是蓝皮阿五，睡眼朦胧的跟着他走。
segs.en: He looked up and saw it was Lanpi Awu, trailing along behind him with sleepy, bleary eyes.

segs.zh: 单四嫂子在这时候，虽然很希望降下一员天将，助他一臂之力，却不愿是阿五。但阿五有点侠气，无论如何，总是偏要帮忙，所以推让了一会，终于得了许可了。他便伸开臂膊，从单四嫂子的乳房和孩子中间，直伸下去，抱去了孩子。单四嫂子便觉乳房上发了一条热，刹时间直热到脸上和耳根。
segs.en: At this moment, although Widow Shan would have welcomed a heavenly warrior to descend and lend her a hand, she certainly did not want it to be Ah Wu. Yet Ah Wu, possessing a touch of chivalry, insisted on helping regardless, and so, after some initial demurral, he finally gained her consent. He stretched out his arms, reaching straight down between Widow Shan’s breast and the child, and lifted the boy away. Widow Shan felt a surge of heat across her breast, which in an instant flushed upward to her face and ears.

segs.zh: 他们两人离开了二尺五寸多地，一同走着。阿五说些话，单四嫂子却大半没有答。走了不多时候，阿五又将孩子还给他，说是昨天与朋友约定的吃饭时候到了；单四嫂子便接了孩子。幸而不远便是家，早看见对门的王九妈在街边坐着，远远地说话：
segs.en: The two of them kept a distance of over two feet apart as they walked along together. Ah Wu kept up a stream of talk, but Sister Shan Si barely replied. After they had walked for a short while, Ah Wu handed the child back to her, saying that it was time for the meal he had arranged with some friends yesterday; Sister Shan Si took the child. Fortunately, her home was not far off, and she could already see Mother Wang from across the street sitting by the roadside, calling out from a distance:

segs.zh: ‘单四嫂子，孩子怎了？——看过先生了么？’
segs.en: Sister Shan Si, what’s wrong with the child? Have you seen a doctor yet?

segs.zh: ‘看是看了。——王九妈，你有年纪，见的多，不如请你老法眼看一看，怎样……’
segs.en: I’ve seen it, yes. But Mother Wang, you’re getting on in years and have seen a great deal; perhaps you wouldn’t mind casting your experienced eye over it and telling us what you think…
Hmm...
How...?

segs.zh: ‘唔……’王九妈端详了一番，把头点了两点，摇了两摇。
segs.en: "Hmm..." Wang Jiuma scrutinized her, nodded twice, then shook her head twice.

segs.zh: 宝儿吃下药，已经是午后了。单四嫂子留心看他神情，似乎仿佛平稳了不少；到得下午，忽然睁开眼叫一声‘妈！’又仍然合上眼，像是睡去了。他睡了一刻，额上鼻尖都沁出一粒一粒的汗珠，单四嫂子轻轻一摸，胶水般黏着手；慌忙去摸胸口，便禁不住呜咽起来。
segs.en: It was already afternoon by the time Bao’er had taken his medicine. Mother Shan watched him closely and thought he seemed a good deal calmer. Then, toward evening, he suddenly opened his eyes and called out, "Ma!" before closing them again, as if drifting off to sleep. After he had slept for a while, beads of sweat broke out on his forehead and the tip of his nose. Mother Shan touched him lightly and found his skin sticky as glue. She hurriedly felt his chest, and could no longer hold back her sobs.

segs.zh: 宝儿的呼吸从平稳变到没有，单四嫂子的声音也就从呜咽变成号咷。这时聚集了几堆人：门内是王九妈、蓝皮阿五之类，门外是咸亨的掌柜和红鼻子老拱之类。王九妈便发命令，烧了一串纸钱；又将两条板橙和五件衣服作抵，替单四嫂子借了两块洋钱，给帮忙的人备饭。
segs.en: Bao’er’s breathing shifted from steady to nonexistent, and Sister Shan Si’s whimpering erupted into a wail. Clusters of people soon gathered: inside the door were the likes of Ninth Aunt Wang and Blue-skinned A-Wu, while outside stood the manager of the Xianheng Tavern and Old Red-Nosed Gong. Ninth Aunt Wang began issuing orders, burning a string of spirit money; she then took two wooden benches and five garments as collateral to borrow two silver dollars for Sister Shan Si, so that a meal could be prepared for those who had come to help.

segs.zh: 第一个问题是棺木。单四嫂子还有一副银耳环和一支裹金的银簪，都交给了咸亨的掌柜，托他作一个保，半现半赊的买一具棺木。蓝皮阿五也伸出手来，很愿意自告奋勇：王九妈却不许他，只准他明天抬棺材的差使，阿五骂了一声‘老畜生’，怏怏的努了嘴站着。掌柜便自去了；晚上回来，说棺木须得现做，后半夜才成功。
segs.en: The first problem was the coffin. Sister Shan Si still possessed a pair of silver earrings and a gold-plated silver hairpin; she handed them both over to the manager of the Xianheng Tavern, entrusting him to act as guarantor so that she might purchase a coffin, partly with cash and partly on credit. Blue-skinned A-Wu reached out a hand, quite eager to volunteer, but Mother Wang Jiu would not allow it, assigning him only the task of helping to carry the coffin the following day. A-Wu cursed her as an "old beast," then stood sulking with his lips pursed. The manager went off on his own; when he returned that evening, he said the coffin would have to be made to order and would not be ready until the latter half of the night.

segs.zh: 掌柜回来的时候，帮忙的人早吃过饭；因为鲁镇还有些古风，所以不上一更，便都回家睡觉了。只有阿五还靠着咸亨的柜台喝酒，老拱也呜呜的唱。
segs.en: By the time the proprietor returned, the assistants had long since finished their meal. Because Lu Town still held to some of its old ways, everyone went home to sleep before the first night watch. Only Ah Wu remained, leaning against the counter of the Xianheng Tavern to drink, while Lao Gong kept up his mournful singing.

segs.zh: 这时候，单四嫂子坐在床沿上哭着，宝儿在床上躺着，纺车静静的在地上立着。许多工夫，单四嫂子的眼泪宣告完结了，眼睛张得很大，看看四面的情形，觉得奇怪：所有的都是不会有的事。他心里计算：不过是梦罢了，这些事都是梦。明天醒过来，自己好好的睡在床上，宝儿也好好的睡在自己身边。他也醒过来，叫一声‘妈’，生龙活虎似的跳去玩了。
segs.en: At this moment, Widow Shan sat on the edge of the bed weeping. Bao’er lay on the bed, and the spinning wheel stood silent on the floor. After a long while, Widow Shan’s tears finally ran dry. She opened her eyes wide and gazed at her surroundings, feeling a sense of bewilderment: none of this could possibly be real. She calculated in her mind: it is nothing but a dream, all of this is just a dream. Tomorrow, when I wake up, I will be sleeping soundly in my bed, and Bao’er will be sleeping soundly beside me. He will wake up too, call out "Ma," and then leap up, full of life and vigor, to go off and play.

segs.zh: 老拱的歌声早经寂静，咸亨也熄了灯。单四嫂子张着眼，总不信所有的事。——鸡也叫了；东方渐渐发白，窗缝里透进了银白色的曙光。
segs.en: Old Gong’s singing had long since fallen silent, and the lights at Xianheng Tavern had been extinguished. Mother Shan, her eyes wide open, still could not bring herself to believe it all. The cock had already crowed; in the east, the sky was gradually paling, and a sliver of silvery dawn light crept through the cracks in the window.

segs.zh: 银白的曙光又渐渐显出绯红，太阳光接着照到屋脊。单四嫂子张着眼，呆呆坐着；听得打门声音，才吃了一吓，跑出去开门。门外一个不认识的人，背了一件东西；后面站着王九妈。
segs.en: The silvery dawn gradually flushed to crimson, and soon the sunlight struck the rooftops. Mother Shan, her eyes wide, sat in a daze. It was only when she heard a knock at the door that she started, then hurried to open it. Outside stood a stranger carrying a bundle on his back, with Old Ninth Aunt Wang standing behind him.

segs.zh: 哦，他们背了棺材来了。
segs.en: Oh, they’ve come, carrying the coffin.

segs.zh: 下半天，棺木才合上盖：因为单四嫂子哭一回，看一回，总不肯死心塌地的盖上；幸亏王九妈等得不耐烦，气愤愤的跑上前，一把拖开他，才七手八脚的盖上了。
segs.en: It was late in the afternoon before the coffin lid was finally nailed shut. Sister Shan Si would weep, then take another look, unwilling to let it be closed once and for all. It was only because Mother Wang had grown impatient and, in a fit of pique, rushed forward to drag her away, that the others were able to scramble and hammer the lid down.

segs.zh: 但单四嫂子待他的宝儿，实在已经尽了心，再没有什么缺陷。昨天烧过一串纸钱，上午又烧了四十九卷大悲咒；收敛的时候，给他穿上顶新的衣裳，平日喜欢的玩意儿，——一个泥人，两个小木碗，两个玻璃瓶，——都放在枕头旁边。后来王九妈掐着指头仔细推敲，也终于想不出一些什么缺陷。
segs.en: As for Mother Shan Si, she had truly done her utmost for her Bao’er; there was not a single thing left wanting. Yesterday she had burned a string of spirit money, and this morning another forty-nine scrolls of the Great Compassion Mantra. When he was laid in his shroud, she dressed him in brand-new clothes, and all the trinkets he had favored in life—a clay figurine, two little wooden bowls, two glass bottles—were placed beside his pillow. Later, Mother Wang Jiu counted on her fingers, pondering it over carefully, but even she could find no fault in what had been done.

segs.zh: 这一日里，蓝皮阿五简直整天没有到；咸亨掌柜便替单四嫂子雇了两名脚夫，每名二百另十个大钱，抬棺木到义冢地上安放。王九妈又帮他煮了饭，凡是动过手开过口的人都吃了饭。太阳渐渐显出要落山的颜色；吃过饭的人也不觉都显出要回家的颜色，——于是他们终于都回了家。
segs.en: That day, Lanpi A-wu did not show his face at all. The proprietor of the Xianheng Tavern hired two porters for Sister Shan Si, paying them two hundred and ten coppers each to carry the coffin to the potter’s field for burial. Wang Jiu-ma helped her cook a meal, and everyone who had lent a hand or offered a word sat down to eat. The sun gradually took on the hue of dusk; those who had finished their meal also began to show signs of wanting to head home—and so, in the end, they all went home.

segs.zh: 单四嫂子很觉得头眩，歇息了一会，倒居然有点平稳了。但他接连着便觉得很异样：遇到了平生没有遇到过的事，不像会有的事，然而的确出现了。他越想越奇，又感到一件异样的事：——这屋子忽然太静了。
segs.en: Sister Shan Si felt quite dizzy, but after resting for a while, she unexpectedly began to steady herself. Yet, one after another, she felt a sense of profound strangeness: she had encountered something she had never experienced in her entire life, something that seemed impossible, and yet it had truly come to pass. The more she pondered, the more bizarre it seemed, and then she became aware of another strange thing: the room had suddenly grown too quiet.

segs.zh: 他站起身，点上灯火，屋子越显得静。他昏昏的走去关上门，回来坐在床沿上，纺车静静的立在地上。他定一定神，四面一看，更觉得坐立不得，屋子不但太静，而且也太大了，东西也太空了。太大的屋子四面包围着他，太空的东西四面压着他，叫他喘气不得。
segs.en: He stood up and lit the lamp; the room seemed only quieter. Dazed, he walked over to shut the door, then returned to sit on the edge of the bed. The spinning wheel stood silent on the floor. He steadied himself and looked about, feeling more restless than ever. The room was not only too quiet, but too vast; the objects in it felt too sparse. The cavernous room hemmed him in on all sides, the emptiness pressing down upon him until he could scarcely draw breath.

segs.zh: 他现在知道他的宝儿确乎死了；不愿意见这屋子，吹熄了灯，躺着。他一面哭，一面想：想那时候，自己纺着棉纱，宝儿坐在身边吃茴香豆，瞪着一双小黑眼睛想了一刻，便说，‘妈——爹卖馄饨，我大了也卖馄饨，卖许多许多钱，——我都给你。’那时候，真是连纺出的棉纱，也仿佛寸寸都有意思，寸寸都活着。但现在怎么了？现在的事，单四嫂子却实在没有想到什么。——我早经说过：他是粗笨女人。他能想出什么呢？他单觉得这屋子太静，太大，太空罢了。
segs.en: She knew now that her Bao'er was truly dead. Unable to bear the sight of the room, she blew out the lamp and lay down. As she wept, she let her thoughts wander back to that time when she would spin cotton, and Bao'er, sitting by her side eating aniseed beans, would stare at her with his little black eyes for a long moment before saying, "Mama—Papa sells wontons, and when I grow up, I’ll sell wontons too. I'll make lots and lots of money—and I'll give it all to you." Back then, even the cotton thread she spun seemed to be imbued with meaning, every inch of it pulsing with life. But what had happened now? As for the present, Sister Shan Si really could not think of anything at all. I have said it before: she was a simple, clumsy woman. What, after all, could she think of? She only felt that the room was too quiet, too large, and too empty.

segs.zh: 但单四嫂子虽然粗笨，却知道还魂是不能有的事，他的宝儿也的确不能再见了。叹一口气，自言自语的说，‘宝儿，你该还在这里，你给我梦里见见罢。’于是合上眼，想赶快睡去，会他的宝儿，苦苦的呼吸通过了静和大和空虚，自己听得明白。
segs.en: But though Sister Shan Si was clumsy and slow, she knew well enough that there was no such thing as a soul returning to its body, and that her Bao’er truly could not be seen again. With a sigh, she spoke to herself, “Bao’er, you must still be around somewhere; come and let me see you in a dream.” Thereupon she closed her eyes, hoping to fall asleep quickly to meet her Bao’er; her labored breathing pierced the stillness and the void, a sound she could hear with painful clarity.

segs.zh: 单四嫂子终于朦朦胧胧的走入睡乡，全屋子都很静。这时红鼻子老拱的小曲，也早经唱完；跄跄踉踉出了咸亨，却又提尖了喉咙，唱道：——
segs.en: At last, Mother Shan Si drifted into a hazy sleep, and the whole house fell silent. By now, Red-Nosed Lao Gong had long since finished his ditty; stumbling out of the Xianheng Tavern, he suddenly strained his voice and began to sing:—

segs.zh: ‘我的冤家呀！——可怜你，——孤另另的……’
segs.en: Oh, my poor soul!—pity you, so lonely...

segs.zh: 蓝皮阿五便伸手揪住了老拱的肩头，两个人七歪八斜的笑着挤着走去。
segs.en: Blue-skinned A-Wu reached out and grabbed Lao Gong by the shoulder, and the two of them went off, weaving and stumbling, laughing and jostling as they walked.

segs.zh: 单四嫂子早睡着了，老拱们也走了，咸亨也关上门了。这时的鲁镇，便完全落在寂静里。只有那暗夜为想变成明天，却仍在这寂静里奔波；另有几条狗，也躲在暗地里呜呜的叫。
segs.en: Mother Shan had long since fallen asleep, the regulars had gone, and the Xianheng Tavern had shut its doors. Lu Town now sank into total silence. Only the dark night, striving to turn into tomorrow, continued to hurry through the stillness; and a few dogs, hidden in the shadows, whimpered to themselves.

segs.zh: （一九二〇年六月。）
